ShortDescription.Value: In-game frame health, sync-point and memory monitor — find which mod stresses your main thread.

Publish.LongDescription:
## Vanilla Profiler
Diagnostic mod for Cities: Skylines II. Two audiences in one install — a traffic-light health overlay for normal players, and a main-thread / sync-point / memory analyzer for mod authors and power users.
### For players
- **Status screen** — Good / Warning / Problem with the likely cause and one-line advice - **Diagnosis screen** — plain-language explanation when something is wrong - **Memory leak detector** — flags sustained managed-heap growth - **Bottleneck verdict** — GpuBound / CpuRenderBound / SimBound / MemoryBound / Balanced with concrete next step - **Optional spike screenshots** — enable with Ctrl+F7 to capture frames over 100 ms to disk - **Ctrl+F11 support file** — one-click bundle (system info, mods, stats, log tail) to send to mod authors
### For mod authors and power users
- **Per-system self main-thread cost** — Stopwatch around every SystemBase.Update separates vanilla vs each mod, subtracts nested system Update time, and keeps inclusive time for diagnostics; assemblies attributed by ModAttribution - **Sync-point flagging** — Update() calls above the threshold (default 0.5 ms, tunable) tagged "[likely sync point]" in the log; per-system suspect-call counter - **ECB.Playback timing** — Harmony hook on EntityCommandBuffer.Playback surfaces structural-change cost separately from system Update time - **GPU/CPU thread breakdown** — Unity ProfilerRecorder for CPU Main / CPU Render / GPU Frame Time (real hardware numbers, not driver-reported) - **Full 5-second report sections** in VanillaProfiler.log — phase tables, top vanilla and mod systems by self main-thread time, inclusive Update context, ECB cost, memory deltas - **Memory metrics** — managed (GC), Mono heap, native alloc/reserved, GPU driver memory, growth rate per second, baseline delta - **Harmony conflict detector** — lists multi-owner Harmony patches only when VanillaProfiler is one of the owners - **Patched-vanilla detector** — when a mod prefixes a vanilla OnUpdate, surfaces the row "Game.X.YSystem N.N ms ← ModName". Total Update ms is measured unconditionally (independent of the "Profile vanilla systems" toggle); the elapsed time blends the patching mod's prefix with the vanilla original because Harmony does not let us split them, but the total is honest. Listed per cycle in the overlay, VanillaProfiler.log report tables, and the Ctrl+F11 export.
### Scope of measurement (read this)
Top per-system numbers reflect **self main-thread cost only** — scheduling overhead, sync points, structural changes, ECB playback, and synchronous main-thread work, with nested SystemBase.Update calls subtracted from the parent. Inclusive/total Update time is still kept for patched-vanilla diagnostics. Job execution on worker threads is **not** captured: Burst-compiled jobs run as native code outside SystemBase.Update() and cannot be instrumented from a mod. For per-job analysis attach **Unity Profiler** to the running game.
Frame time, GPU frame time, CPU main/render thread time, all memory metrics, frame spike detection and sync-point flagging are accurate.
### Hotkeys
- **Ctrl+F7** — Toggle spike screenshots - **Ctrl+F8** — Settings panel - **Ctrl+F9** — Cycle overlay mode (Status → Diagnosis → Tips → Details → Engine → Hide) - **Ctrl+F10** — Force log dump - **Ctrl+F11** — Create a support file (system info, mods, stats, log tail) - **Ctrl+F12** — Cycle overlay position
### Output locations
- Continuous log: `Logs/VanillaProfiler.log` - Diagnostic reports: `Reports/CSII_Report_*.txt` - Support bundles: `Reports/CSII_Report_*.zip` - Spike screenshots: `Logs/spikes/spike_*.png` - Settings: `VanillaProfiler/settings.json`
All paths are inside `%LOCALAPPDATA%Low\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\`.
### Compatibility
- **Works with any mod combination** — no gameplay changes, no entity creation, no save persistence - **Read-only ECS queries** — never mutates game state - **Detects Harmony patch conflicts** — lists methods patched by more than one mod
### Performance overhead
Adds approximately **15 microseconds per frame** from Stopwatch instrumentation across ~300 systems. Negligible compared to the systems being measured.
### How to share a bug report
1. Press **Ctrl+F11** while the lag is happening 2. Find `CSII_Report_*.zip` and `CSII_Report_*.txt` in your Reports folder 3. Attach the `.zip` when possible, otherwise attach the `.txt` — it includes system info, loaded mods, current settings, the latest report window, top mods, and the last 50 lines of VanillaProfiler.log
Publish.ChangeLog:
Internal refactoring of profiler architecture — no behavior changes for end users.
Report building, health classification, graphics settings reflection, and metrics bucket ownership are now explicit components with typed measurements, typed metrics buckets, typed overlay rows, immutable health reports, and typed recorder values; aggregation, output, and overlay code no longer pass loosely related string/bool/value tuples. Overlay main panel renderer and settings draft are split into separate components. Report formatting and log tail reading are split into independent modules. Memory recorders are extracted into a dedicated MemoryRecorderSet. Mod attribution responsibilities are split across smaller focused components.
